template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how tool {tl:tool.name} uses a technique that works on Windows and another that works only on Linux, both techniques having the same course of action.
Answer: Tool {tl.name} uses two different techniques, {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}. Both techniques are mitigated by mitigation {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name}<*INVISIBLE: {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name} constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}*>. Technique {t1.name} works on platforms (inclding Windows): {t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']} and technique t2 works only on platform <* ONLY LINUX*>{t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).
template_object.text-short: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}, both mitigated by mitigations (same!) {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}. Constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}. t1 platforms(?Win)={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique t2 platform(*Linux) {t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).

query:
MATCH (tl:tool), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t1:`attack-pattern`), (tl:tool)-[:uses]->(t2:`attack-pattern`), (t1:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa1:`course-of-action`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa2:`course-of-action`), (coa1:`course-of-action`), (coa2:`course-of-action`), (t1:`attack-pattern`), (t2:`attack-pattern`)
WHERE coa1.public_id = coa2.public_id AND elementId(t1) <> elementId(t2) AND ("Windows" IN t1.x_mitre_platforms) AND ALL(x IN t2.x_mitre_platforms WHERE x="Linux")
LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT tl.name, t1.name, t2.name, coa1.name, coa2.name, coa1.public_id, coa2.public_id, t1.x_mitre_platforms, t2.x_mitre_platforms
ORDER BY rand()
